sections.title: Part 1
sections.passage: You will hear a conversation between a man and a woman about the Buckworth Conservation Group.

questionGroups.title: Buckworth Conservation Group
questionGroups.instructions: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

content.text:
**Regular activities**

**Beach**
● making sure the beach does not have

content.question: making sure the beach does not have ______ on it

content.text:
on it
● no

**Nature reserve**
● maintaining paths
● nesting boxes for birds installed
● next task: taking action to attract

content.question: next task: taking action to attract ______ to the place

content.text:
to the place
● identifying types of

content.question: identifying types of ______

content.text: ● building a new

content.question: building a new ______

content.text:
**Forthcoming events**

**Saturday**
● meet at Dunsmore Beach car park
● walk across the sands and reach the

content.question: walk across the sands and reach the ______

content.text:
● take a picnic
● wear appropriate

content.question: wear appropriate ______

content.text:
**Woodwork session**
● suitable for

content.question: suitable for ______ to participate in

content.text:
to participate in
● making

content.question: making ______ out of wood

content.text:
out of wood
● 17th, from 10 a.m. to 3 p.m.
● cost of session (no camping): £

content.question: cost of session (no camping): £______

sections.title: Part 2
sections.passage: You will hear a tour guide called Lou Miller talking to people about a boat trip round Tasmania.

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of people who can stand on each side of the boat?
What colour are the tour boats?

options.text: dark red
jet black
light green

questions.question: Which lunchbox is suitable for someone who doesn't eat meat or fish?

options.text: Lunchbox 1
Lunchbox 2
Lunchbox 3

questions.question: What should people do with their litter?

options.text: take it home
hand it to a member of staff
put it in the bins provided on the boat

questionGroups.question: Which TWO features of the lighthouse does Lou mention?

options.text: why it was built
who built it
how long it took to build
who staffed it
what it was built with

questionGroups.question: Which TWO types of creature might come close to the boat?

options.text: sea eagles
fur seals
dolphins
whales
penguins

questionGroups.question: Which TWO points does Lou make about the caves?

options.text: Only large tourist boats can visit them.
The entrances to them are often blocked.
It is too dangerous for individuals to go near them.
Someone will explain what is inside them.
They cannot be reached on foot.

sections.title: Part 3
sections.passage: You will hear two veterinary science students, Diana and Tim, discussing their work experience on farms and the modules on their course.

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: What problem did both Diana and Tim have when arranging their work experience?

options.text: making initial contact with suitable farms
organising transport to and from the farm
finding a placement for the required length of time

questions.question: Tim was pleased to be able to help

options.text: a lamb that had a broken leg.
a sheep that was having difficulty giving birth.
a newly born lamb that was having trouble feeding.

questions.question: Diana says the sheep on her farm

options.text: were of various different varieties.
were mainly reared for their meat.
had better quality wool than sheep on the hills.

questions.question: What did the students learn about adding supplements to chicken feed?

options.text: These should only be given if specially needed.
It is worth paying extra for the most effective ones.
The amount given at one time should be limited.

questions.question: What happened when Diana was working with dairy cows?

options.text: She identified some cows incorrectly.
She accidentally threw some milk away.
She made a mistake when storing milk.

questions.question: What did both farmers mention about vets and farming?

options.text: Vets are failing to cope with some aspects of animal health.
There needs to be a fundamental change in the training of vets.
Some jobs could be done by the farmer rather than by a vet.

questionGroups.instructions: What opinion do the students give about each of the following modules on their veterinary science course? Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–F, next to questions 27–30.

options.text: Tim found this easier than expected.
Tim thought this was not very clearly organised.
Diana may do some further study on this.
They both found the reading required for this was difficult.
Tim was shocked at something he learned on this module.
They were both surprised how little is known about some aspects of this.

sections.title: Part 4
sections.passage: You will hear a lecture about labyrinths.

questionGroups.title: Labyrinths
questionGroups.instructions: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

content.text:
**Definition**
● a winding spiral path leading to a central area

**Labyrinths compared with mazes**
● Mazes are a type of

content.question: Mazes are a type of ______
______ is needed to navigate through a maze

content.text:
is needed to navigate through a maze
● the word 'maze' is derived from a word meaning a feeling of

content.question: the word 'maze' is derived from a word meaning a feeling of ______

content.text:
● Labyrinths represent a journey through life
● they have frequently been used in

content.question: they have frequently been used in ______ and prayer

content.text:
and prayer

**Early examples of the labyrinth spiral**
● Ancient carvings on

content.question: Ancient carvings on ______ have been found across many cultures

content.text:
have been found across many cultures
● The Pima, a Native American tribe, wove the symbol on baskets
● Ancient Greeks used the symbol on

content.question: Ancient Greeks used the symbol on ______

content.text:
**Walking labyrinths**
● The largest surviving example of a turf labyrinth once had a big

content.question: The largest surviving example of a turf labyrinth once had a big ______ at its centre

content.text:
at its centre

**Labyrinths nowadays**
● Believed to have a beneficial impact on mental and physical health, e.g., walking a maze can reduce a person's

content.question: walking a maze can reduce a person's ______ rate

content.text:
rate
● Used in medical and health and fitness settings and also prisons
● Popular with patients, visitors and staff in hospitals
   - patients who can't walk can use 'finger labyrinths' made from

content.question: patients who can't walk can use 'finger labyrinths' made from ______

content.text: - research has shown that Alzheimer's sufferers experience less

content.question: research has shown that Alzheimer's sufferers experience less ______
